title: F2-prep: shared banner-i18n .cjs library with readFabricLanguage helper + 6-banner string table
description: Create a NEW shared CommonJS library that (a) reads `fabric_language` from `.fabric/fabric-config.json` synchronously with the same never-throw contract as the existing config helpers in fabric-hint.cjs, and (b) exports a string-table renderer keyed by signal id (archiveLine1, archiveActivity, archiveCta, reviewLine1, reviewCta, importLine1, importCta, maintenanceLine2, maintenanceLine1Never, maintenanceLine1Aged, broadImportBanner) returning rendered text for the four supported variants (`zh-CN`, `en`, `zh-CN-hybrid`, `match-existing`). `match-existing` and unknown values default to `en` per UX i18n Policy class 1. Protected tokens MUST be passed through verbatim regardless of variant: slash command names (`/fabric-archive`, `/fabric-review`, `/fabric-import`), CLI commands (`fabric doctor --lint`, `fab install`), file paths, the leading `📋 Fabric:` emoji prefix, and all numeric/template substrings the existing tests assert on (e.g. `${hoursElapsed.toFixed(1)}h`, `阈值 ${N}`, `${count} 条`, `${nodeCount}/${threshold}`, `${days} 天`).

files.change: Export `readFabricLanguage(workspaceRoot)` (returns one of 'zh-CN'|'en'|'zh-CN-hybrid'|'match-existing', defaulting to 'match-existing' on missing/malformed config) AND `renderBanner(signalKey, variant, params)` (returns rendered string; falls back to 'en' table for variant in {'match-existing', unknown})

implementation: Mirror the synchronous fs+JSON.parse pattern used by the existing CONFIG_FILE readers in fabric-hint.cjs (lines 80-90, 712+) — never throw, swallow errors, return default

Define `STRINGS` object with keys for each of the 11 distinct banner fragments (5 from fabric-hint Signals A/B/C/D + 1 from broad hook + variants thereof), each mapping variant -> template function `(params) => string`
Implement `renderBanner(key, variant, params)` that resolves variant via `STRINGS[key][variant] ?? STRINGS[key]['en']` then invokes the template fn
For `zh-CN-hybrid` variant: use Chinese narrative copy but preserve English protected tokens verbatim (slash commands, CLI commands, file paths inline-quoted)
Export `module.exports = { readFabricLanguage, renderBanner, STRINGS }` (STRINGS exported for test introspection)

reference.pattern: Existing CONFIG_FILE readers in fabric-hint.cjs (lines 80-90, 712+) for the never-throw fs read; existing IMPORT_RECOMMENDATION_BANNER constant in knowledge-hint-broad.cjs:262 for the banner-text shape
reference.examples: session-digest-writer.cjs is the existing precedent for a hooks/lib/*.cjs shared module — follow the same require-relative-path layout

criteria: File `packages/cli/templates/hooks/lib/banner-i18n.cjs` exists and is requirable from `../fabric-hint.cjs` and `../knowledge-hint-broad.cjs`
`renderBanner('archiveLine1', 'en', { hoursElapsed: 25.0, archiveHintHours: 24 })` contains substrings `'25.0h'` and `'24h'` (substring contract preserved)
`renderBanner('archiveLine1', 'zh-CN-hybrid', ...)` contains both Chinese characters AND verbatim `/fabric-archive` token
Test: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- banner-i18n` passes (added in TASK-004)

rationale.chosen_approach: New standalone .cjs lib (mirrors session-digest-writer.cjs precedent) rather than reusing TS i18n.ts because hooks run as raw .cjs scripts at runtime — they cannot import from @fenglimg/fabric-shared
alternatives_considered: Inline string tables in each .cjs hook (rejected: 6 banners across 2 files = duplication)
Bundle TS i18n into hooks at install-time (rejected: increases install complexity, breaks the standalone .cjs contract)
JSON-only string table loaded at runtime (rejected: template substitution needs JS functions)
decision_factors: Hook .cjs files must remain dependency-free standalone scripts
Test substring contracts (e.g. '25.0h', '/fabric-archive') must survive translation
Single source of truth for 4 variants × 11 fragments
rationale.tradeoffs: Adds a second hooks/lib file the install pipeline must copy alongside the hook scripts — install/skills-and-hooks.ts will need a verification check that lib/banner-i18n.cjs ships with each hook install (handled in TASK-002 verification)

manual_checks: Manually require the lib from a Node REPL with the workspace root and inspect that the four-variant fallback works
success_metrics: 100% of 11 banner keys render non-empty for all 4 variants
Zero throws across 1000 fuzzed (workspaceRoot, variant) combinations
